insights.headline: Two Drive files contain the wrong artist's data entirely
insights.detail: "KATSEYE - EU/UK Tour 2026" actually contains BTS/aespa/TWICE/BLACKPINK tracking tables, not KATSEYE data, and "The Strokes - UK/EU 2026 Tour" duplicates the aespa tracker instead of Strokes data. Both were kept and flagged (is_comparable_reference/anomaly) rather than discarded, but the underlying files need to be re-saved with correct content before they're usable for KATSEYE or Strokes reporting.

insights.headline: J.Cole's Johannesburg date is the highest EDP figure in the entire dataset
insights.detail: FNB Stadium, Johannesburg (12 Dec 2026) hit 672,585 EDP views at 72h - more than double BTS's Madrid opener (232,181) and Harry Styles' Amsterdam record (381,879) in the baseline set. Worth a sanity check on this figure given how far it sits above every other show, but if accurate it's a standout demand signal for J.Cole's only African date, and the dataset's first non-Europe/UK/Ireland data point.

insights.headline: BTS Stade de France queue data looks copy-pasted across both dates
insights.detail: The onsale peak queue for both Paris dates (17 and 18 Jul 2026) is identical at ~66,300 down to every timestamp column in the source queue-tracking file - a near-impossible coincidence that suggests one row was duplicated rather than reflecting real per-date figures. Flag for whoever maintains "BTS - RELEASES EU/UK 2026".

insights.headline: Don Toliver's EU/UK tour sheet has a systemic ADP row-offset bug
insights.detail: At least four shows (Berlin, Lodz, Amsterdam, Hamburg) show ADP figures labelled with a country that doesn't match the show's actual market (e.g. a Berlin show's ADP row labelled TM_NL), consistent with the ADP country column being shifted by a few rows relative to the EDP show list. This affects a meaningful chunk of the tour's ADP data and is worth fixing at the source rather than per-row.

insights.headline: Gracie Abrams' "2026" tour file is entirely 2027 dates
insights.detail: Every show in "Gracie Abrams UKEU Tour 2026" falls between April and May 2027, not 2026 as the filename implies. All 25 records from this file are flagged anomaly:true for the mismatch - worth a filename correction to avoid future confusion.

insights.headline: Allianz Arena (Munich) gets its first real per-show data points
insights.detail: Previously the venue baseline had only a single aggregated megastar data point with no queue detail. BTS's two Munich dates now give real onsale peak queues of 185,701 (11 Jul) and 128,067 (12 Jul) - both below the baseline's combined onsale figure of 219,382, suggesting that baseline figure may have summed both dates rather than representing a single-show peak.

insights.headline: BTS Tottenham Hotspur Stadium pacing sits just under the venue's 'extraordinary event' range
insights.detail: The venue baseline for megastar general-onsale queues at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium is 220k-533k (from Beyonce's 2023 dates). BTS's two London dates peaked at 195,640 (6 Jul) and 177,873 (7 Jul) - strong, but just under that historical extraordinary-demand band, with the usual second-night rolloff pattern.

insights.headline: Olivia Rodrigo's O2 London comparable dwarfs Gracie Abrams' own O2 shows
insights.detail: A benchmark block pasted into the Gracie Abrams sheet shows Olivia Rodrigo hitting 86,721 EDP views at 72h for her 5 Apr 2027 O2 London date, roughly 10x Gracie Abrams' own O2 London peak of 8,613 (1 May 2027) at the same venue - a useful tier-gap illustration between a top-of-market pop headliner and a strong mid-tier act.

insights.headline: J.Cole outpaces Drake's Co-op Live comparable figures
insights.detail: J.Cole's Co-op Live Manchester date (28 Oct 2026) reached 80,234 EDP views at 72h, well above the pasted Drake 2025 Tour benchmark for the same venue (13,354-20,500 EDP at 24h). Time windows differ so this isn't a like-for-like comparison, but it's a strong directional signal on relative demand for the two artists at the same room.

insights.headline: Two likely data-entry errors in the Kodaline tour sheet
insights.detail: Carlswerk Victoria, Cologne shows a 72h EDP figure (188) lower than its own 48h figure (414), which shouldn't happen for a cumulative view count. Roundhouse London shows an implausible ~29x jump from 389 (48h) to 11,222 (72h). Both were kept as-is with notes rather than corrected, but likely need a source-sheet fix.

insights.headline: Shakira's ES Residency shows a steep, consistent night-on-night decline
insights.detail: EDP views at 72h drop from 36,255 (25 Sep) to 25,709 (26 Sep) to 8,465 (27 Sep) across three consecutive Madrid residency dates - a clean front-loaded demand curve typical of a residency opener, useful as a reference pattern for pacing multi-night runs.

insights.headline: BTS's Madrid opener lines up exactly with the existing venue baseline
insights.detail: BTS's 26 Jun 2026 Riyadh Air Metropolitano date shows 232,181 EDP views at 72h, matching the venue-baselines.json BTS entry for this venue exactly (232,181) - a good cross-validation signal that this sync's extraction is accurate for at least this show.
